ground_truth.title: Replacement Projector Recommendations 2024
ground_truth.author: JosephF
ground_truth.main_content:
JosephF
Jan 3, 2024

I have a quite old JVC RS2 in my basement home theater that has been an absolutely wonderful projector for me. But the time has come to start looking at a replacement that can handle 4K.

I'm too cheap to stick with JVC and even though it is getting long in tooth the Epson 5050UB seems to be a ton of bang for the buck. With the RS2 I have obviously not been in the projector market for quite some time. What are some other options that people would recommend that I take a look at before making a decision?

As for use, I'm not much of a gamer (play a little Assetto Corsa from time to time) and mostly use the RS2 to watch movies, car racing (F1, Indy, NASCAR, etc.) and other sports.

eljaycanuck
Jan 3, 2024

Best Selling Home Theater Projectors under $3000
www.projectorcentral.com

JosephF
Jan 3, 2024

I did run across that, and it has value, but I tend to like to hear what actual owners have to say :)

bud16415
Jan 3, 2024

I'm just curious as I'm similar to you in I enjoy my stuff way longer than most here and have been also speculating on that I'm really missing out on something not updating sooner.

What do you feel you are missing with the JVC RS2? I also have an old 1080p DLP and every time I watch it still I remark how great it looks and when I go out for a movie (seldom anymore) I regret not renting the BD or streaming and staying to watch it.

To answer your question in part everyone I read that has the 5050UB has good things to say.

JosephF
Jan 3, 2024

bud16415 said:
What do you feel you are missing with the JVC RS2?

Probably FOMO more than anything else

More seriously I've had my setup for quite awhile now and would be looking at replacing not only the projector, but adding a UHD Blu Ray player, a new Pre/Pro or AVR that supports 4K and adding height speakers. Part of the drive is that for the last several years when I buy Blu Rays I've opted for the ones that include 4K but can't watch them in my theater!

Reddig
Jan 3, 2024

I went from a JVC RS540 to an Epson 5050UB. My JVC had bit the dust. I'm really enjoying the 5050. It's brighter than the JVC but blacks aren't quite as good. Overall I love the projector and have been enjoying it very much. I couldn't afford a new JVC.

jedi1982
Jan 3, 2024

I had an Epson 5040UB for 6 years (2017-2023) until we sold our house and am going to the JVC NZ9 for the new house. The 5040UB is the gen prior to 5050. Loved the image for 4k and even HDR. The likely issue for you coming from JVC would be the difference in blacks. That being said, I do believe you will be pleased with the overall image!

tom9933
Jan 3, 2024

If you are interested in a used machine I have a JVC 990 (calibrated by ChadB with his HDR tweaks) that got a new bulb shortly before getting replaced by my LTD... Before the 990 I had an Epson 5030 which I purchased initially due to its "brighter" numbers. As others have mentioned the JVC blacks are significantly better and I would have a hard time going back even though the Epson is a great bang for buck machine.

JosephF
Jan 3, 2024

Well I didn't just dip the toe in.

I dove in headfirst and ordered an Epson 5050UB, Denon AVR-X3700h that I'll use as a pre/pro, a Panasonic UB820 and some Monoprice cables

rekbones
Jan 4, 2024

I have a batcave but I dislike the goofing around that comes with every turn on cycle of bulb projectors.
I went with a no fuss his3nse c1 laser projector.
I prefer no hassle type projectors.

JosephF
Jan 3, 2024

Our older daughter bought a house early last year. So I'm guessing her basement may inherit the JVC :)

AV_Integrated
Jan 4, 2024

Asks about new projector to buy in 2024...
Buys a projector released in 2019.

When I complain that the front projection industry artificially is holding back movement in the technology, I don't think I'm wrong to do so.

BenQ showed that adding a solid state light source only costs a few hundred bucks. It is LONG overdue for the 3200, 3800, 4010, and 5050 to get a refresh.

JosephF
Jan 4, 2024

AV_Integrated said:
Asks about new projector to buy in 2024...
Buys a projector released in 2019.

To be fair I'm still using a projector released in, if I recall correctly, 2007. And before that a CRT that was released in 1990!

Richard239
Jan 4, 2024

Release date isn't everything. My Pioneer Kuro from 2008 still has a better picture than the newer TVs I have. Technology is not always linear. I bought one of the newer Epson projectors this year, and the blacks were totally gray. I returned it. It was apparently brighter than the old model, but it sacrificed black levels for brightness. I had assumed a newly released Epson would be better than the older model it was replacing. Some people may prefer the brighter projector based on the lighting conditions in their viewing environment, not me.

AV_Integrated
Jan 4, 2024

sigh - I miss my Pioneer.

Yes, I'm well aware of how release date isn't everything. I'm more concerned about the snail's pace that we are getting new products in the projector world. It's artificial. A modern 5050UB with the same lens, and similar chip, with a laser light source and updated video processing should already be out. Not the LS12000, but something at $3,000. I kind of feel like the LS12000/LS11000 are the replacements for the 4010/5050 models, but are priced at least $1,000 higher than they should be.

It all feels super artificial to me. Epson is cashing in (as they should!) on the fact that DLP has been incredibly stagnant over the past few years with their stuff and Sony and JVC are well outside the budget of most.

Alvy200
Jan 4, 2024

NEC NP P502HG

Richard239
Jan 4, 2024

I agree with the fact that by now Epson should have updated the 5050ub. I just didn't want the original poster to think they bought some really old model that's no good. It's a great projector. It's a shame the new Epson laser projectors do not support 3D, or I would have bought one. The 5050ub may be the last great projector from Epson that supports 3D. It's still a great purchase today.

OzzieP
Sep 22, 2024

I went from a JVC RS540 to an Epson 5050UB. My JVC had bit the dust. I'm really enjoying the 5050. It's brighter than the JVC but blacks aren't quite as good. Overall I love the projector and have been enjoying it very much. I couldn't afford a new JVC.

ggery
Sep 26, 2024

Try formovie theater from nothingprojector.com. They have a promotion going on their website (there was no such promotion when I bought it). Buy a projector and get a screen for free (worth more than 600 euros). This projector is a 4K laser projector. I bought it from them once and I'm using it now. I've been using it for five months and everything is good.
